title: Energex — Public safety
investigation_objective: Paid a penalty (reported as $60,000 in one AER source; an earlier related release cites $100,000/$140,000 total across the two distributors — treat the exact split as MEDIUM pending direct document confirmation) for alleged breaches of life support customer obligations under the National Energy Retail Rules — obligations that exist specifically to protect customers dependent on electricity supply for life support equipment.
scope: Alleged breach of life support customer obligations under the National Energy Retail Rules, resulting in an AER penalty and administrative undertaking
